home.eyebrow: The secondary market, honest for once
home.heroTitle: Verified tickets. Face value. Zero worries.
home.heroSubtitle: The secondary-resale platform that connects ticket holders with buyers — through the provider's own transfer, with funds held in escrow, at the original face value only.
home.heroCardTitle: Search any event. Only verified listings.
home.trustVerifiedBody: Every ticket is verified with the provider before it's listed — no surprises at the door.
home.trustFaceValueBody: Sellers can't set the price — you pay the original face value, not a shekel more.
home.trustOfficialTransferBody: Tickets move through the provider's own system — no PDFs, no WhatsApp, no guessing.
home.trustEscrowBody: The seller gets paid only after the transfer is confirmed on your end. No risk.

home.howItWorksStep2Body: Your money sits in escrow until the official transfer reaches you.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.intro: Five steps. No surprises, no PDFs, no WhatsApp.
howItWorks.connectBody: The seller connects their provider account (Ticketmaster, Le'an, Eventim, Hadran) to Safe Ticket via OAuth. We see only the tickets — never credit or payment details.
howItWorks.verifyBody: Before a ticket is listed, we pull it directly from the provider and confirm it's valid, at face value, and that the seller is the owner.
howItWorks.listBody: The seller picks an event and a specific seat. The price is set from the provider's record — the seller cannot raise it by a single shekel.
howItWorks.transferBody: Once the buyer pays, the ticket moves through the provider's system — straight to the buyer's account. No PDF, no photo, no emailed QR code.
howItWorks.payBody: Funds stay in escrow until the buyer confirms the ticket landed in their provider account. Only then is the seller paid.
howItWorks.refundBody: Once the official transfer is complete, the ticket belongs to the buyer for every purpose — cancellations, refunds, and reschedules are handled directly by the original ticket provider, not through Safe Ticket.

faq.title: Frequently asked
faq.intro: The things people ask us most often. Can't find your answer? Get in touch.
faq.q1: How do I know the ticket is real?
faq.a1: Every ticket listed on Safe Ticket is pulled directly from the official provider's system (Ticketmaster, Le'an, Eventim, or Hadran) before it appears in search. We don't accept PDF uploads or photos — only tickets verified through the provider's system.
faq.q2: Why can't the seller charge more than face value?
faq.a2: That's the whole point of Safe Ticket. We connect sellers who can't make an event with buyers who want to — not scalpers. Prices are locked to the provider's original face value, and sellers can't change them.
faq.q3: When does my money get released to the seller?
faq.a3: Funds sit in an escrow account from the moment of payment until you confirm the ticket has landed in your provider account. Only then does the seller get paid. If anything goes wrong, your money comes back.
faq.q4: What if the transfer from the provider fails?
faq.a4: If the transfer can't complete on time for any reason (say, a provider outage), the sale is automatically cancelled and you get a full refund. You won't be left paid up with no ticket.
faq.q5: Does Safe Ticket refund cancelled events?
faq.a5: If an event is cancelled outright, the official provider handles the refund on their own policy. Once the transfer is complete, Safe Ticket no longer holds the money — refunds come from the provider, not us.
faq.q6: What if the event is rescheduled?
faq.a6: If an event is rescheduled, the ticket is valid for the new date (standard Israeli practice). If you can't make the new date, you can relist the ticket on Safe Ticket and resell it at face value.
faq.q7: How much does the service cost?
faq.a7: Buyers pay a small service fee (typically 5–8% of face value), always shown separately from the ticket price. Sellers pay a similar fee against what they receive. We always split the fee out — there's no markup or hidden cost.
faq.a8: Today: Ticketmaster Israel, Le'an, Eventim, and Hadran — the four largest providers in the Israeli market. We add more as we complete secure integrations.
faq.a9: We collect the minimum needed to run the service — name, email, phone, and payment method. We don't get access to your full ticket wallet at the provider, only the specific ticket listed. The full privacy policy is on its dedicated page.
faq.a10: Safe Ticket focuses on the Israeli market — events take place in Israel and transfers happen within Israeli provider accounts. Buyers abroad can purchase if they hold a valid Israeli provider account.
